goto-meeting.name: GoTo Meeting
goto-meeting.short_description: GoTo Meeting is a longstanding video conferencing platform with HD video, screen sharing, cloud recording, and AI meeting transcription.
goto-meeting.starting_price: From $12 per organizer/month, billed annually (Professional plan)

features.description: Host meetings with high-definition video and clear audio for participants joining from a browser, desktop app, or mobile device.
features.title: HD video and audio conferencing

features.description: Share a full desktop, single application window, or browser tab so participants can follow a presentation, demo, or document review.
features.title: Screen sharing

features.description: Automatically records meetings, generates transcripts, and produces AI-written summaries that highlight key topics and action items.
features.title: Smart Meeting Assistant (AI transcription and summaries)

features.description: Record sessions to the cloud so they can be reviewed, shared, or transcribed after the meeting ends without local storage management.
features.title: Cloud recording

features.description: Mark up a shared screen in real time with pointers, shapes, and freehand drawing to guide participants through a walkthrough.
features.title: Drawing and annotation tools

features.description: Every organizer gets a fixed, reusable meeting link and dial-in number, so recurring meetings don't need a new invite each time.
features.title: Personal meeting room

features.description: Company admins can manage organizer licenses, lock meetings, control entry, and review account-wide usage from a central dashboard.
features.title: Admin and security controls

features.description: Native apps for Windows, Mac, iOS, and Android let hosts and participants start or join meetings without relying on a browser.
features.title: Mobile and desktop apps

features.description: Schedule and launch meetings directly from Outlook or Google Calendar without switching between separate scheduling tools.
features.title: Calendar integrations

pros: One of the most established, reliable video conferencing products, with a long track record for call stability
Smart Meeting Assistant provides useful AI transcripts and summaries without needing a separate note-taking tool
Simple, low-friction joining experience for guests, including browser-based join and phone dial-in options
Fits naturally into the broader GoTo Connect suite for businesses already using GoTo for phone or IT support
Centralized admin controls make it easier to manage licenses and security across larger organizer counts
cons: No permanent free plan, only a time-limited free trial, unlike several competing video conferencing tools
Entry-level Professional plan caps participants at 50, which is limiting for larger team meetings
Not designed for large public webinars or virtual events, which require GoTo's separate webinar product
Interface feels dated next to newer collaboration-first tools with deeper chat and whiteboard features
Less tightly integrated into Microsoft Teams or Google Workspace than each platform's own native meeting tool

faqs.answer: No. GoTo Meeting does not offer a permanent free tier; it offers a 14-day free trial of its paid plans, after which a Professional, Business, or Enterprise subscription is required to keep hosting meetings.
faqs.question: Does GoTo Meeting have a free plan?

faqs.answer: Capacity depends on the plan: the Professional plan supports up to 50 participants, the Business plan up to 250, and the Enterprise plan up to 3,000 participants per meeting.
faqs.question: How many people can join a GoTo Meeting session?

faqs.answer: Yes. Its Smart Meeting Assistant automatically records and transcribes meetings and generates an AI summary highlighting key discussion points and action items, included with Business and Enterprise plans.
faqs.question: Does GoTo Meeting include AI transcription and meeting summaries?

faqs.answer: No. GoTo Meeting is designed for standard meetings and client calls, while GoTo Webinar is a separate product built for large-scale webinars with registration pages and audience marketing tools.
faqs.question: Is GoTo Meeting the same product as GoTo Webinar?

faqs.answer: GoTo Meeting is available through a web browser and has dedicated desktop apps for Windows and Mac, plus mobile apps for iOS and Android.
faqs.question: What platforms does GoTo Meeting run on?

features: Smart Meeting Assistant (AI transcripts and summaries)

restream.name: Restream
restream.short_description: Restream is a multistreaming platform for broadcasting live video to YouTube, Twitch, Facebook, LinkedIn, and other platforms simultaneously.
restream.starting_price: Free plan available; paid plans start at $16/month billed annually ($19/month billed monthly)
restream.headquarters: Austin, Texas, USA (originally founded in Vinnytsia, Ukraine)

features.description: Send one live video source to dozens of destinations at once, including YouTube, Twitch, Facebook, LinkedIn, and TikTok, without running separate broadcasts.
features.title: Multiplatform simultaneous broadcasting

features.description: A browser-based, no-download production studio for switching scenes, adding branded overlays, and hosting multiple on-screen participants.
features.title: Restream Studio

features.description: Invite remote guests onto a virtual set and, on higher tiers, give team members shared access to manage broadcasts together.
features.title: Guest and team collaboration

features.description: Aggregates comments and reactions from every connected platform into one unified chat feed for hosts and moderators.
features.title: Restream Chat

features.description: Automatically records every broadcast to the cloud for later download, review, or repurposing into other content.
features.title: Cloud recording

features.description: Reports viewership and engagement metrics across all connected channels from a single view.
features.title: Analytics dashboard

features.description: Apply custom overlays, logos, and scene layouts to broadcasts so streams match a creator's or brand's visual identity.
features.title: Custom branded layouts

features.description: An add-on that automatically extracts short highlight clips from recorded broadcasts for repurposing on social platforms.
features.title: AI-assisted clip generation

features.description: Accepts an incoming stream from external encoders like OBS or vMix and fans it out to multiple destinations.
features.title: RTMP and SRT ingest

features.description: Lets developers and larger organizations automate stream setup and channel configuration programmatically.
features.title: Public API

pros: Free plan lets creators try multistreaming before paying for anything
Broad platform coverage, reaching dozens of destinations from one broadcast
Browser-based Studio requires no software installation
Restream Chat unifies audience interaction across every connected platform
Scales from solo creators on the free tier up to teams on Business or Enterprise plans
cons: Free plan is limited to two channels and includes Restream branding
Higher resolution, longer recording retention, and guest features require paid tiers
AI clip generation is a separate paid add-on rather than included by default
Broadcast quality depends on a stable connection to Restream's servers
Enterprise-level features require contacting sales instead of transparent published pricing

faqs.answer: Restream is used to broadcast one live video feed to multiple platforms at the same time, such as YouTube, Twitch, Facebook, and LinkedIn, instead of streaming to each platform separately.
faqs.question: What is Restream used for?

faqs.answer: Yes, Restream offers a free plan that supports multistreaming to two channels at once, though it includes Restream branding and fewer features than the paid tiers.
faqs.question: Does Restream have a free plan?

faqs.answer: Yes, Restream accepts an incoming RTMP or SRT stream from external encoders like OBS or vMix and distributes it to the connected destination platforms.
faqs.question: Can I use Restream with OBS or vMix?

faqs.answer: No installation is required to use Restream Studio, since it runs directly in a web browser; using an external encoder like OBS is optional rather than required.
faqs.question: Does Restream require any software to be installed?

faqs.answer: Paid plans start with the Standard tier at $16 per month when billed annually, moving up through Professional and Business tiers, with Enterprise pricing available by contacting sales.
faqs.question: How much do Restream's paid plans cost?

features: Website player for large concurrent audiences

taskwarrior.name: Taskwarrior

nocodb.short_description: NocoDB is a no-code database platform that turns spreadsheets or existing SQL databases into collaborative, self-hostable apps.

features.description: Grid, Gallery, Form, Kanban, and Calendar views over the same underlying data.

features.description: Linked records, lookups, rollups, and formula fields with create/read/update/delete operations.

features.description: Point NocoDB at an existing Postgres or MySQL database instead of migrating data into its own storage.

features.description: Every base gets REST APIs, webhooks, and an SDK for programmatic access and automation.
features.title: Auto-generated REST APIs

features.description: Fine-grained permissions across five user roles.

features.description: Built-in connectors for Slack, Discord, AWS SES/S3, SMTP, and other services.

Both cloud-hosted and self-hosted deployment options, with a genuine free cloud tier
cons: The self-hosted Community Edition moved from AGPLv3 to the Sustainable Use License, a source-available license rather than an OSI-approved open-source license, which drew criticism from parts of the self-hosting community

SSO, audit logs, and row-level security are reserved for Business/Scale/Enterprise tiers

faqs.answer: Yes. NocoDB offers a free cloud plan (3 editor seats, 1,000 records) and a free, self-hosted Community Edition.

faqs.answer: The self-hosted Community Edition is distributed under the Sustainable Use License, a source-available license, after the project moved away from AGPLv3.

faqs.answer: Yes, NocoDB can turn an existing Postgres or MySQL database into a spreadsheet-style interface rather than requiring data import.
faqs.question: Can NocoDB connect to an existing database?

faqs.answer: Yes, every base gets auto-generated REST APIs, webhooks, and an SDK.
faqs.question: Does NocoDB provide an API?

faqs.answer: Yes, via Docker, binary installers for macOS/Linux/Windows, or an install script.
faqs.question: Is NocoDB available self-hosted?
